articles.title: Supply Chain Security Crisis: Too Many Vulnerabilities, Too Little Visibility
articles.summary: New vulnerabilities are being discovered too fast, the time-to-exploitation is too short, and our visibility into them is largely lacking. The post Supply Chain Security Crisis: Too Many Vulnerabilities, Too Little Visibility appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Microsoft warns of new Defender zero-days exploited in attacks
articles.summary: On Wednesday, Microsoft started rolling out security patches for two Defender vulnerabilities that have been exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]

articles.title: 9-Year-Old Linux Kernel Flaw Enables Root Command Execution on Major Distros
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in the Linux kernel that remained undetected for nine years. articles.title: GitHub links repo breach to TanStack npm supply-chain attack
articles.summary: GitHub says the hackers who breached 3,800 internal repositories gained access via a malicious version of the Nx Console VS Code extension, compromised in last week's TanStack npm supply-chain attack. [...]

articles.title: GitHub Internal Repositories Breached via Malicious Nx Console VS Code Extension
articles.summary: GitHub on Wednesday officially confirmed that the breach of its internal repositories was the result of a compromise of an employee device involving a poisoned version of the Nx Console Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extension.  The development comes as the Nx team revealed that the ext

articles.title: Highly Critical Drupal Core Flaw Exposes PostgreSQL Sites to RCE Attacks
articles.summary: Drupal has released security updates for a "highly critical" security vulnerability in Drupal Core that could be exploited by attackers to achieve remote code execution, privilege escalation, or information disclosure. The vulnerability, now tracked as CVE-2026-9082, carries a CVSS score of 6.5 out

articles.title: Europe dismantles VPN service used by cybercriminals to hide ransomware attacks
articles.summary: The international operation targeted a service known as First VPN, which had been marketed for years on Russian-speaking cybercrime forums as a secure way for criminals to evade law enforcement.

articles.title: Ukraine identifies infostealer operator tied to 28,000 stolen accounts
articles.summary: The Ukrainian cyberpolice, working in conjunction with U.S. law enforcement, has identified an 18-year-old man from Odesa suspected of running an infostealer malware operation targeting users of an online store in California. [...]

articles.title: Hackers bypass SonicWall VPN MFA due to incomplete patching
articles.summary: Threat actors brute-forced VPN credentials and bypassed multi-factor authentication (MFA) on SonicWall Gen6 SSL-VPN appliances to deploy tools used in ransomware attacks. [...]

articles.title: Ukraine probes teen suspect in cyber theft scheme targeting California online shoppers
articles.summary: The investigation began after U.S. authorities informed their Ukrainian counterparts that hackers operating from Ukraine could be involved in attacks targeting users of American e-commerce platforms, Ukraine's Prosecutor General said.

articles.title: 7-Eleven confirms breach after ShinyHunters claims
articles.summary: The breach notification letters say 7-Eleven discovered the breach on April 8 and, after an investigation, determined that the cybercriminals gained access to “certain 7-Eleven systems used to store franchisee documents.”

articles.title: Grafana breach caused by missed token rotation after TanStack attack
articles.summary: The Grafana data breach was caused by a single GitHub workflow token that slipped through the rotation process following the TanStack npm supply-chain attack last week. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft Rolls Out Mitigations for ‘YellowKey’ BitLocker Bypass
articles.summary: The exploitation is mitigated by preventing the FsTx Auto Recovery Utility from starting when the WinRE image launches. The post Microsoft Rolls Out Mitigations for ‘YellowKey’ BitLocker Bypass appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: AI-Powered App Attacks Are Faster, More Frequent and Harder to Stop
articles.summary: Digital.ai’s latest threat report warns that agentic AI has erased the distinction between emerging and primary targets, enabling attackers to strike mobile apps within hours of release across every industry. The post AI-Powered App Attacks Are Faster, More Frequent and Harder to Stop appeared first

articles.title: Microsoft Takes Down Malware-Signing Service Behind Ransomware Attacks
articles.summary: Microsoft on Tuesday said it disrupted a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) operation that weaponized the company's Artifact Signing system to deliver malicious code and conduct ransomware and other attacks, compromising thousands of machines and networks across the world. articles.title: Identity Alone Isn't Enough: Why Device Security Has to Share the Load
articles.summary: Identity checks alone can't stop attackers using stolen session tokens and compromised devices. Specops Software outlines why Zero Trust strategies increasingly depend on continuous device verification. [...]

articles.title: Anthropic Silently Patches Claude Code Sandbox Bypass
articles.summary: The researcher who found it says the vulnerability could have been chained with a prompt injection to exfiltrate data. The post Anthropic Silently Patches Claude Code Sandbox Bypass appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Drupal critical update to fix bug with high exploitation risk
articles.summary: Drupal has announced a "core security release" scheduled for later today, warning that threat actors might develop exploits within hours of the update disclosure. [...]

articles.title: Webworm Deploys EchoCreep and GraphWorm Backdoors Using Discord and MS Graph API
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged fresh activity from a China-aligned threat actor known as Webworm in 2025, deploying custom backdoors that employ Discord and Microsoft Graph API for command-and-control (C2 or C&C) communications. Webworm, first publicly documented by Broadcom-owned Symant

articles.title: GitHub confirms being hacked by TeamPCP, says customer data unaffected
articles.summary: Github, which hosts code for more than 100 million developers worldwide, confirmed the breach on social media after TeamPCP advertised stolen source code on a cybercrime forum.

articles.title: Senator presses CISA for answers about alleged GitHub repository leak
articles.summary: U.S. Senator Maggie Hassan (D-NH) sent a letter to the acting director of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday demanding answers about an alleged breach uncovered by cybersecurity reporter Brian Krebs involving government contractor Nightwing.

articles.title: CISA Adds Seven Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
articles.summary: CISA has added seven new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. articles.title: GitHub Breached — Employee Device Hack Led to Exfiltration of 3,800+ Internal Repos
articles.summary: GitHub on Tuesday said it's investigating unauthorized access to its internal repositories after the notorious threat actor known as TeamPCP listed the platform's source code and internal organizations for sale on a cybercrime forum. "While we currently have no evidence of impact to customer informa

articles.title: Over 320 NPM Packages Hit by Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: A compromised maintainer account was used to publish malicious package versions across the @antv namespace. The post Over 320 NPM Packages Hit by Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Exploit released for new PinTheft Arch Linux root escalation flaw
articles.summary: PinTheft, a recently patched Linux privilege escalation vulnerability, now has a publicly available proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit that allows local attackers to gain root privileges on Arch Linux systems. [...]

articles.title: Virtual Event Today: Threat Detection & Incident Response Summit
articles.summary: Don't miss this virtual event as we explore how to cut through alert fatigue, leverage AI and unified platforms to accelerate investigations, and apply actionable threat intelligence. The post Virtual Event Today: Threat Detection & Incident Response Summit appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: GitHub Confirms Hack Impacting 3,800 Internal Repositories
articles.summary: The TeamPCP hacking group accessed the repositories after a GitHub employee installed a poisoned VS Code extension. The post GitHub Confirms Hack Impacting 3,800 Internal Repositories appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: Microsoft on Tuesday released a mitigation for a BitLocker bypass vulnerability named YellowKey following its public disclosure last week. articles.title: GitHub confirms breach of 3,800 repos via malicious VSCode extension
articles.summary: GitHub has confirmed that roughly 3,800 internal repositories were breached after one of its employees installed a malicious VS Code extension. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft shares mitigation for YellowKey Windows zero-day
articles.summary: Microsoft has shared mitigations for YellowKey, a recently disclosed Windows BitLocker zero-day vulnerability that grants access to protected drives. [...]

articles.summary: Grafana Labs, on May 19, 2026, said an investigation into its recent breach found no evidence of customer production systems or operations being compromised. articles.title: GitHub investigates internal repositories breach claimed by TeamPCP
articles.summary: GitHub is investigating a breach of its internal repositories after the TeamPCP hacker group claimed to have accessed approximately 4,000 repositories containing private code. [...]

articles.title: Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Overtakes Credential Theft as Top Breach Vector
articles.summary: Verizon’s 2026 DBIR finds vulnerability exploitation has overtaken credential abuse as the leading breach vector, as AI accelerates attacks, patching delays worsen, and ransomware and third-party compromises continue to surge. articles.title: Ukraine says Russia is deploying AI-powered malware on the battlefield
articles.summary: A new report from Ukraine’s National Security and Defense Council says Russia’s use of AI across cyber operations expanded dramatically over the past year, reshaping everything from social engineering campaigns to malware development and creating what Ukrainian officials describe as a growing imbala

articles.title: Max-severity flaw in ChromaDB for AI apps allows server hijacking
articles.summary: A max-severity vulnerability in the latest Python FastAPI version of the ChromaDB project allows unauthenticated attackers to run arbitrary code on exposed servers. [...]

articles.title: Cybercrime service disrupted for abusing Microsoft platform to sign malware
articles.summary: Microsoft says it has disrupted a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) operation that abused the company's Artifact Signing service to generate fraudulent code-signing certificates used by ransomware gangs and other cybercriminals. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft Self-Service Password Reset abused in Azure data theft attacks
articles.summary: A threat actor targeting Microsoft 365 and Azure production environments is stealing data in attacks that abuse legitimate applications and administration features. [...]

articles.title: Huawei zero-day attack behind last year’s crash of Luxembourg's entire telecoms network
articles.summary: There is no evidence that the incident has recurred, but the flaw remains unexplained and has not been publicly acknowledged by the company.

articles.title: Trapdoor Android Ad Fraud Scheme Hit 659 Million Daily Bid Requests Using 455 Apps
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new ad fraud and malvertising operation dubbed Trapdoor targeting Android device users. The activity, per HUMAN's Satori Threat Intelligence and Research Team, encompassed 455 malicious Android apps and 183 threat actor-owned command-and-control

articles.title: Microsoft disrupts Fox Tempest malware-signing-as-a-service platform tied to ransomware gangs
articles.summary: The company unsealed a legal case in U.S. District Court on Tuesday detailing the disruption of Fox Tempest — a popular service that has operated since May 2025 and provides cybercriminals with code signing tools.

articles.title: Drupal to Patch Highly Critical Vulnerability at Risk of Quick Exploitation

articles.title: Microsoft Disrupts Malware-Signing Service Run by ‘Fox Tempest’
articles.summary: Fox Tempest provides a service that cybercriminals use to distribute ransomware and other malware disguised as legitimate software. The post Microsoft Disrupts Malware-Signing Service Run by ‘Fox Tempest’ appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: New Shai-Hulud malware wave compromises 600 npm packages
articles.summary: Threat actors earlier today published more than 600 malicious packages to the Node Package Manager (npm) index as part of a new Shai-Hulud supply-chain campaign. [...]

articles.title: 7-Eleven confirms data breach claimed by the ShinyHunters gang
articles.summary: Convenience store chain giant 7-Eleven confirmed that its systems were breached in a cyberattack claimed by the ShinyHunters extortion group last month. [...]

articles.title: Critical Microsoft Vulnerabilities Doubled: From Exposure to Escalation
articles.summary: Microsoft's total vulnerability count stayed steady in 2025, but critical flaws surged year over year. BeyondTrust breaks down why attackers are increasingly focused on privilege escalation and identity abuse. [...]
